synthesis.summary: ENPH is up 10.4% today and 45% over the past five days on 2.1x normal volume, but no news items were supplied to explain the move. The overbought RSI (80) and unusual volume suggest strong momentum-driven or options-related buying, but without a catalyst in the news feed, the driver cannot be identified from available information.

EVC dropped 11.78% today with no news in the supplied feed to explain the move. Relative volume is only modestly elevated (1.08x), suggesting this isn't a broad liquidity event, and the RSI was extended at 66.8 heading into the session, leaving a technical pullback or unseen order flow (options expiry, institutional selling) as the most likely drivers.

FIG is up ~13% on 3x relative volume with no news in the supplied feed to explain the move. The price action and volume surge suggest a catalyst exists — possibly undisclosed M&A activity, options flow, or peer rotation — but nothing in the available news supports a fundamental explanation.

FRMI dropped 11.75% today with no news in the supplied feed to explain the move. Relative volume is below average (0.88x), which rules out an obvious catalyst-driven event; the drop more likely reflects profit-taking or unwinding after a sharp 24% 5-day run-up.

KOS is up 11% today with no supplied news to explain the move. Relative volume is below average (0.72x), which rules out a clear catalyst-driven surge; the move is more likely driven by options flow, short covering, or sector rotation in small-cap energy names.

POET fell ~21% today on elevated volume, but no news items were supplied to explain the drop. Given the stock is still up ~48% over the past 5 days, this looks like a sharp mean-reversion pullback after a significant run-up, possibly driven by profit-taking or options flow — not any identifiable fundamental catalyst.

RUM dropped 11.44% today with no supplied news to explain the move. The modestly elevated relative volume (1.3x) and bearish MACD cross suggest selling pressure, but without a identifiable catalyst this looks like options flow, broader small-cap risk-off rotation, or undisclosed selling — not a news-driven event.

SATL is up 14.4% today and 31% over five days on nearly double its average volume, but no news items were supplied to explain the move. The surge into overbought RSI territory (70+) could reflect options flow, short covering, or sector rotation — none of which can be confirmed without additional data.

RXT dropped nearly 20% on roughly 2.7x normal volume with no news in the supplied feed to explain the move. The sharp decline with elevated volume suggests a catalyst exists — possibly an earnings release, guidance cut, or large block trade — but none is visible in available inputs.

SEDG is up 23% today and nearly 50% over five days on 2.5x normal volume, but no news items were supplied to explain the move. This could reflect short-squeeze dynamics, sector rotation into beaten-down solar names, or options-driven momentum — none of which are visible in the available data.

SHLS is up 11% today with no news in the supplied feed to explain the move. The 16.9% 5-day gain, elevated relative volume (1.47x), and overbought RSI (71) suggest a momentum-driven surge, possibly from short covering, sector rotation into solar/energy names, or options flow — none of which are visible in the available data.

SG surged 17.4% with 1.77x relative volume and no news in the supplied feed to explain the move. The catalyst is not identifiable from available information — this could be options flow, short covering, or sector rotation that isn't captured here.

TNGX dropped 17.34% on 2.6x normal volume with no supplied news to explain the move. This could reflect clinical trial data, a regulatory update, pipeline setback, or institutional selling, but none of those catalysts are present in the available news feed.

WOLF is down 11.4% today with no news to explain the drop. After a 33% run over the prior five days, the stock was technically overbought (RSI ~75), making a sharp pullback consistent with profit-taking or mean reversion — but no supplied news item provides a fundamental catalyst.

YSS dropped 17% today and is down 31% over the past five days on elevated volume, but no news items were supplied to explain the move. The selloff could reflect sector rotation, large holder liquidation, or options-driven pressure — none of which are visible in the available data.

INTC dropped 6% today with no supplied news to explain the move. The relatively low relative volume (0.66) suggests this isn't a broad panic selloff, but the 5-day loss of nearly 13% indicates sustained selling pressure over the week. Without any news catalyst in the input, this could reflect sector rotation, options flow, or macro pressure on semiconductors — none of which can be confirmed here.

KO is up 0.45% today with below-average volume (0.7x), suggesting routine price drift rather than a news-driven move. No supplied news items explain the gain, and the modest magnitude is consistent with sector rotation or passive index flows.

NVDA dropped 4.44% on below-average volume (rel. volume 0.86), but none of the supplied news items provide a credible explanation — the two unique stories cover ETF product launches with no direct read-through to NVDA's fundamentals. The move likely reflects broader sector rotation, options flow, or macro pressure not captured in today's news feed.

RKLB dropped ~6% today with no news to explain the move. The pullback follows an 18% 5-day run and an RSI above 71, suggesting profit-taking or mean-reversion pressure after an overbought condition rather than any fundamental catalyst.

XOM is up 4.1% on elevated volume, but the available news doesn't fully account for the move. A Wall Street analyst calling a commodity supercycle provides a tailwind narrative for energy majors like Exxon, but an analyst thematic call alone rarely drives a 4%+ single-day move of this magnitude in a mega-cap stock. No company-specific catalyst — earnings beat, guidance change, M&A, or production update — is present in the news set.
